items.original_title: Britain Blocks Drug Firm From Supplying Flu Vaccine
items.original_body: The company that makes half the flu vaccine used in the U.S. was suspended for manufacturing problems.

items.original_body: (CBS/AP) Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan said Wednesday the economy has quot;regained some traction quot; after a late spring slowdown that was triggered by a sharp spike in oil prices.

items.original_body: Microsoft wants to release its Longhorn client operating system in 2006 in order to quot;appease quot; the customers that have bought into its Software Assurance licensing package, according to analyst Gartner.

items.original_title: Radcliffe Ready to Take Bite Out of Big Apple
items.original_body: NEW YORK (Reuters) - World record holder Paula Radcliffe will race back into the spotlight at the New York Marathon Sunday when she returns to action for the first time since her stunning collapse at the Athens Olympic Games.

items.original_title: Alaska Oil Spill Takes Toll on Animals and Fisheries
items.original_body: A rare break in rough Bering Sea weather is allowing officials to gain a better sense of damage from a large spill in the Aleutian Islands.

items.original_title: Nice Day for a Wireless Wedding
items.original_body: Even though we knew it was coming, journalists seem to have plenty to write about now that underdogs Sprint and Nextel have announced their \$35 billion nuptial plans, including a rumor that one of the industry's big dogs would try to spoil the party. -washingtonpost.com
